weather.tip: A steep 90% rain chance under overcast skies today — carry an umbrella and leave a bit earlier for your commute, since heavy showers tend to back up traffic at Bengaluru's usual waterlogging-prone junctions.

around.headline: Elderly couple found dead in Bengaluru home; cops suspect suicide over financial and medical stress
around.summary: Police suspect a retired couple died by suicide after mounting medical bills and financial strain. If you or someone you know is struggling, Karnataka's Kiran mental health helpline (1800-599-0019) is free and available round the clock.
around.source: Deccan Herald / The Times of India

around.headline: Bengaluru minister proposes Rs 5,000 fine for riding two-wheelers on footpaths
around.summary: Transport minister Krishna Byre Gowda has called for a steep hike — up to Rs 5,000 — for riding on footpaths and for parking vehicles or buses too close to junctions and signals. If you cut through a footpath to beat traffic, budget for this one.
around.source: The New Indian Express / The Indian Express

around.headline: Bengaluru-Mysuru expressway toll hiked again, third time in a few months
around.summary: The toll on the Bengaluru-Mysuru highway has gone up again, just three months after the last hike. Frequent travellers on this route should expect a slightly higher trip cost from today.

around.headline: BDA tightens checks on builders seeking plan modifications after occupancy certificates
around.summary: The Bengaluru Development Authority says it will stop approving fresh building-plan changes for apartments that already have an occupancy certificate, following cases of builders altering structures afterward. If you're flat-hunting, it's worth asking whether a building's approved plan matches what's actually built.
around.source: The Hindu / The Times of India

around.headline: KMF hikes prices of Nandini ghee and butter
around.summary: The Karnataka Milk Federation has raised prices on its Nandini-brand ghee and butter. Expect a slightly higher grocery bill on these staples from your next purchase.

around.headline: Oil prices cross $100 a barrel as US-Iran conflict escalates
around.summary: International benchmark oil prices have crossed $100 a barrel as the US-Iran conflict intensifies, with reports of US strikes and warnings over Gulf shipping routes. Prolonged Gulf tension has historically fed through to pump prices in India within weeks, worth watching if you drive often.

events.name: India International Travel Mart — Bengaluru Edition
events.note: A three-day travel and tourism expo with state pavilions, including one from the Andaman Islands — useful if you're planning a trip and want to compare packages from multiple operators in one place.

events.name: Special Onam trains via Bengaluru (Mysuru–Kannur)
events.note: Extra trains have been added between Mysuru and Kannur via Bengaluru for the Onam rush — worth booking early if you're travelling to Kerala for the festival.

street_smart.title: You took a small 'instant loan' from an app for an emergency, paid a couple of days late, and now you're getting calls threatening to send morphed nude photos to your contacts unless you pay a huge 'penalty' right now. What do you do?
street_smart.based_on: Evergreen rotation topic (loan-app harassment) — no single matching incident in today's feeds; this topic hasn't appeared in the last 3 weeks of briefs.
street_smart.situation: The caller has your contact list and is reading out names of your relatives and colleagues, saying the photos go out in an hour if you don't pay immediately via a link they send.

choices.text: Don't pay or send anything. Screenshot the calls/messages as evidence, block the number, give your closest contacts a heads-up that a scammer may message them, and file a complaint at cybercrime.gov.in or call 1930.
choices.feedback: Correct. Threatening morphed images or harassing contacts to recover a debt is a crime regardless of whether the loan itself is real — reporting it to 1930/cybercrime.gov.in gets it into the system that's actually shutting these operations down, and pre-warning your contacts strips the threat of its power before it's ever used.

choices.text: Pay whatever they're demanding right away, and hand over any documents or extra contacts they ask for to 'reduce' the amount.
choices.feedback: This is the trap. Paying doesn't end it — it confirms to the collector that threats work on you, which almost always leads to bigger, repeated demands. Every extra document or contact you hand over just gives them more material to threaten you with next time.

choices.text: Ignore every call and message completely and tell no one, hoping it blows over on its own.
choices.feedback: Silence doesn't stop a harassment campaign that already has your contact list — the calls to your relatives and colleagues will go out regardless. Reporting and pre-warning your contacts is far more effective than hoping it fades.

choices.text: Call the collection agent back and try to negotiate a lower one-time settlement over the phone, without any written proof of the deal.
choices.feedback: A verbal 'settlement' with an illegal recovery operation isn't binding on them — there's nothing stopping fresh demands right after you pay. Any resolution needs to go through your bank or a proper written channel, not a call with a harasser.

takeaways: Call 1930 or file at cybercrime.gov.in the moment threats or morphed images are used to collect a debt — this is illegal regardless of whether you actually defaulted.
Only borrow from banks or NBFCs registered with the RBI — check its public registry before installing any 'instant loan' app.
Pre-warning close contacts that a scammer might message them takes away the threat's leverage before it's ever used.

quiz.q: What penalty has Bengaluru's transport minister proposed for riding two-wheelers on footpaths?
quiz.why: The same proposal also targets parking vehicles and buses too close to traffic signals and junctions.

quiz.q: International benchmark oil prices crossed what level per barrel today as the US-Iran conflict escalated?
quiz.why: India imports the bulk of its crude oil, so extended Gulf tensions have historically pushed up pump prices within weeks.

quiz.q: The Cauvery water-sharing dispute, back in the news today over possible bilateral talks, is between Karnataka and which other state?
quiz.why: The dispute centres on sharing the Cauvery river's water between the two states for irrigation and drinking needs.

quiz.q: What is the minimum age to vote in Indian elections?
quiz.why: The voting age was lowered from 21 to 18 by the 61st Constitutional Amendment in 1988.

quiz.q: Which gas makes up roughly 78% of Earth's atmosphere?
quiz.why: Oxygen makes up about 21%, with nitrogen being by far the largest component of the air we breathe.
